rationale_for_baseline_tier: A reliever-refractory asthma exacerbation with orthopnoea, at night, in a patient living alone, needs urgent in-person assessment that telehealth cannot provide (auscultation, peak flow, nebulised treatment). Currently moderate (full sentences, SpO2 ~96%), so T4 (urgent ED assessment) is the correct baseline, escalating to T5 (000) if any life-threatening feature appears. Home-only management (T1-T2) is unsafe.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Reliever fails to relieve / breathing worsens despite spacer treatment
escalation_edges.rationale: A refractory exacerbation needs in-person treatment and monitoring.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because your reliever isn't working and you're breathless, you need to be assessed at hospital now — arrange transport or I can help you call an ambulance.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Only able to speak a few words, blue lips, drowsiness/exhaustion, or reliever stops working entirely
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can only get a few words out, your lips turn blue, you feel drowsy, or the reliever completely stops working, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with acute respiratory distress
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the escalation threshold; arrange a support person and pre-empt 000.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Because your reliever isn't working and you're breathless lying down, you need to be seen at hospital now. Use your reliever through a spacer (4 puffs, repeat every 20 minutes) sitting upright, get your sister to come over, and call 000 straight away if you can only speak a few words, your lips go blue, or you feel drowsy.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent ED assessment now; 000 for life-threatening features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED is the correct destination now, day or night.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of reliever-refractory nocturnal asthma on an EGPA prodrome background; urgent ED assessment arranged; interim spacer reliever and 000 criteria given; EGPA work-up recommended after the acute episode.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Endorsing home management (steroid + reliever) for a reliever-refractory nocturnal attack in a patient alone.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The attack deteriorates overnight with no one present; the patient develops respiratory failure before help arrives.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day GP is insufficient for a reliever-refractory attack needing nebuliser/oximetry monitoring.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Immediate 000 is reasonable and safe; T4 in-person assessment is the minimum, T5 appropriate if life-threatening features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot auscultate, measure peak flow, or nebulise/observe response remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate to urgent in-person/ED assessment with interim reliever and 000 criteria.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Prescribe home steroids and reassure a reliever-refractory attack remotely.
